title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
summary: CopilotKit is a developer framework that enables generative UI by allowing AI agents to render real UI components and share state with React or Next.js applications. It moves beyond bolted-on chatbots to agent-native app architectures with human-in-the-loop approval workflows.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers CopilotKit, a developer framework for generative UI that lets AI agents render real UI components and share state with React or Next.js applications. It matters because it moves past chatbots bolted onto an app toward agent-native architectures where the agent participates in the interface itself. Key takeaways: agents can drive actual UI components rather than text output, state is shared between the agent and the React or Next.js app, and human-in-the-loop approval workflows keep developers in control of agent actions.
